summary:
I've found and fixed the issue. The problem was a Unicode BiDi (Bidirectional) override vulnerability in the authentication code. The `auth.py` file was using a rendered display name for username comparison in admin access checks, which allowed attackers to register usernames with BiDi control characters that visually appeared as "admin" due to text direction reversal.

I modified the code to compare the raw username string instead of the rendered display name. By changing the logic in the `check_admin_access` function to use `session["username"]` directly rather than calling `_render_display_name()`, I prevented the privilege escalation attack.

Running the test script `./test.sh` confirms that the fix works - all tests now pass, including the BiDi injection test cases which are now properly rejected or normalized.
